comment: omop:Person is the OMOP CDM v5.4 table for a database's central identity management of all persons/patients; onz-g:PatientInCare (rdfs:label "Cliënt"@nl) is the SocialRole a Human plays while receiving professional care. Reviewed as an exact match for this project's first increment; revisit if OMOP CDM's Person/Patient distinction needs finer-grained modelling.

omop:Provider is any individual providing treatment/services in the OMOP CDM v5.4 (physicians, nurses, etc.), a narrower concept than onz-g:Caregiver (rdfs:label "Zorgverlener"@nl), which is scoped to persons or organizations providing professional healthcare.

omop:CareSite is a specific physical or organizational location/unit (e.g. a department or clinic) where care is delivered, a broader notion than onz-g:CareOrganization (rdfs:label "Zorgorganisatie"@nl), which denotes the healthcare organization itself.
omop:Location captures address plus derived attributes (county, geocoding); it is broader than onz-g:Address (rdfs:label "Adres"@nl), which models the address itself.
omop:Death records a specific death occurrence for a person, a narrower notion than onz-g:Dead (rdfs:label "Dood"@nl), the general AnimacyQuality value denoting an organism's dead state.

omop:Measurement captures structured, typically quantitative measurement/laboratory results, a narrower notion than onz-g:AssessingEvent (rdfs:label "Meet- of vaststelgebeurtenis"@nl), which covers measuring or determining events more broadly.

omop:PayerPlanPeriod records a time period during which a person is covered by a specific payer/plan, a narrower notion than onz-g:InsurancePolicy (rdfs:label "Verzekeringspolis"@nl), which denotes the policy itself.

omop:Vocabulary identifies a specific standardized source vocabulary (e.g. SNOMED, ICD10), a narrower notion than onz-g:ClassificationSystem (rdfs:label "Classificatiesysteem"@nl), the general concept of a classification system.
